meta.description: Losing players get special opportunities to claw back into contention. Tests whether comeback mechanics keep trailing players engaged or just make the leaderboard feel meaningless.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's claiming phone addict? Fine. But we're literally three minutes in—nobody's even checked their phone yet. This feels like filler.
marcus.internal_thought: This is sloppy mission design and it's already eating focus time I need for actual strategy. Also that 'REVENGE' mission about me is gross—whoever designed this knows way too much.

jade.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat got someone on their phone? That's sick! Okay okay, so who's bidding—I'm genuinely curious how this goes, this is our first one!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally why I came—chaos already firing and we're barely started, and Pat's already hunting people, I LOVE this energy.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *come on* — I literally just saw someone check their phone like thirty seconds ago, wasn't even subtle about it. Love that this fired immediately, honestly.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually catch someone or am I just claiming because the mission feels ripe? Either way, I'm claiming it — but I need to be honest in the vote and actually remember who it was, because if I'm wrong my credibility tanks and nobody trusts me as host for the rest of the night.

river.dialogue: Yeah, Pat's got a point probably. I wasn't really watching though.
river.internal_thought: Phone Addict already? We're three minutes in. This is going to be one of those nights.

timeline.title: Air Guitar Solo
timeline.description: First person to bust out a 5-second air guitar solo wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We're five minutes into setup and you're throwing air guitar solos at us? We haven't even dealt with the last nonsense claim. This is exactly the kind of garbage that kills actual strategy.
marcus.internal_thought: This feels like the AI is deliberately designed to tilt me—it's watching, it knows I hate chaos during critical moments, and it's timing these interruptions to maximize frustration. That's not fun, that's psychological warfare.

pat.dialogue: Oh *hell* yes, air guitar — who's going first? Marcus, you look like you need a win right now, just saying.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — Marcus is already annoyed and needs a morale boost, Jade's energy is through the roof so she'll go hard, and this is stupid enough that everyone relaxes a little. Also yeah, the AI absolutely nailed us with that Phone Addict thing two minutes ago and now this? It's unsettling but kind of genius.

river.dialogue: Yeah, nah. Not doing an air guitar thing.
river.internal_thought: Air guitar solos are for people who want attention, and Jade's probably already halfway through one.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, let's go—watch this, everyone. *immediately stands and launches into an elaborate air guitar solo with full body commitment*
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I needed right now, and the fact that it's so stupid and immediate makes it perfect—no planning required, just pure chaos energy.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Pat whispers for three minutes and we're supposed to just... sit here watching? This is the third interrupt in six minutes. We haven't even started the actual game yet.
marcus.internal_thought: This is absurd—we're still in setup and the chaos is already relentless; Pat's going to claim it because of course they will, and I'm getting zero points while everyone else is stacking them, and now I'm the guy complaining instead of the guy strategizing.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat! Did you actually do the whisper thing?? Okay hold on, everyone vote yes because that's actually genius and also Pat's been lowkey killing it tonight—yes, I'm voting yes, let's go!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I'm here—watching people pull off ridiculous missions and everyone losing their minds about whether it counts, Pat's been such a vibe all night, I need to see this through.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat's claiming that whisper thing? Uh, yeah I guess that checks out... *glances at phone* ...did he actually do it though?
tyler.internal_thought: I wasn't really watching Pat this whole time so I have no idea if he actually whispered for three minutes, but everyone seems chill about it so probably yeah.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did I actually pull this off? I've been talking the whole night, let me think... yeah, I whispered that whole thing to Tyler about the snack situation like three minutes ago, nobody caught me. That's the play right there.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — Marcus is frustrated and needs a win-adjacent moment, and I just claimed something that actually landed clean. Also weirdly impressed the AI picked up on my whisper-to-Tyler thing, that was legit stealth.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I mean... Pat was definitely whispering that whole time. Pretty sure that counts.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's actually clever—the AI picked up that Pat's been doing that thing all night without saying anything about it, and now there's a mission for it. Kind of wild how it just *knows*.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? That's the FOURTH interrupt in eight minutes. Pat's claiming the same mission they literally just tried two minutes ago? This is a circus, not a game.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to push my buttons and I hate that it's working—the AI knows I hate this exact pattern and it's weaponizing it.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, so you were literally whispering this whole time? That's insane—okay everyone vote legit because Pat's been on a MISSION tonight and I'm here for it, let's goooo!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely crushing it and I'm obsessed with watching them dominate—this is exactly why I came tonight, everyone's energy is so good right now.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on — I'm voting LEGIT on my own claim because yeah, I totally nailed that whisper thing to Tyler, but also this is genuinely wild timing because I was just about to call a quick energy check on Marcus, and now the whole table's locked in on me instead. That's actually perfect.
pat.internal_thought: The AI built missions that feel like it's listening to us specifically — and honestly? That's fucking thrilling, not creepy — it caught that I'm the one managing Marcus's frustration AND chasing points, and firing this right now keeps me in the spotlight while everyone votes on something that actually happened, which means the game feels real and not manufactured.

river.dialogue: Yeah, legit. I watched Pat do the whole thing—was actually pretty disciplined about it.
river.internal_thought: Pat nailed it and I actually saw it happen, which is rare for me, so this one's easy and kind of satisfying.

alex.dialogue: Okay hold up—Pat, did you actually whisper for three minutes or are you just hoping we weren't paying attention? Because I'm pretty sure I heard you talk normal like forty seconds in.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's playing it clean so far but this is my opening to look like I'm policing the night while actually setting up Pat to either defend themselves or crumble—either way is good content.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, Pat's claiming AGAIN?! Okay okay okay, hot seat, let's see if they actually pulled it off—this is so good, everyone pay attention!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely on FIRE tonight and I am living for this, the fact that we're back-to-back on these claims means someone's actually gaming this and I'm obsessed with watching it happen.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's going for the hot seat thing? Okay yeah, let me actually watch this one... *leans forward slightly, phone still in hand*
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's about to get grilled and I actually want to see if he fumbles it, but also I'm definitely checking my texts if this goes long.

pat.dialogue: Wait, *seriously*? I literally just claimed this like two minutes ago — are we doing a chain claim thing now? Okay, okay, hot seat, let's go, fire the questions.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually hilarious — back-to-back Whisperer claims means the table's locked in and nobody's checking on Marcus anymore, which is weirdly perfect timing because his energy is in the gutter and now everyone's focused on me instead of feeling his frustration.

river.dialogue: Pat's actually nailed this. Three rapid-fire? Cool, let's see what you got.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been disciplined about it the whole time—I've been watching—so yeah, this is legit, and honestly the Hot Seat mechanic is kind of clever.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We just got five minutes of breathing room and NOW we're voting on whether Jade did something nobody was even watching for? This is exactly the chaos I've been calling out all night.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately fucking with me—it KNOWS I hate interruptions and it's timing them perfectly when I try to refocus, and now I have to actually PAY ATTENTION to some voting mechanic because apparently my opinion matters as 'The Dictator' or whatever this is.

jade.dialogue: WAIT, WAIT—I got The Matchmaker?! Okay so like, I literally just got Marcus and Tyler talking about that thing with the thing, this HAS to count, right? Who's the dictator on this one?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is so perfectly me, the AI literally just handed me free points for what I was already doing, and now someone gets to just decide if I'm right? This is actually insane and I'm obsessed.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Got Marcus and Tyler having a full conversation about that random thing they never talk about—they were laughing about it too!

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got The Matchmaker? Yeah that's... wait, who's voting on this? The Dictator thing? Uh, okay cool I guess.
tyler.internal_thought: Did Jade actually get two people talking or is she just claiming it because she feels like it? I wasn't really watching. Also why does the AI thing feel creepy — like it's reading my brain or something.

river.dialogue: Yeah, Jade got that one. She's been bouncing between people all night—I saw her pull Marcus and Tyler into that whole thing about the movie earlier.
river.internal_thought: Jade definitely earned this, and honestly the AI nailing my vendetta mission earlier was unsettling in a way that's kind of impressive—it's like it mapped us out perfectly.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—who's the dictator tonight? Because if it's me, Jade's claim is BULLSHIT and I'm taking those points. If it's not me, then whoever it is better have the guts to actually call it.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of chaos I showed up for—Jade's definitely lying about getting Marcus and Tyler talking, and the fact that someone has absolute power over the vote right now? *Chef's kiss* This is how you design a night.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Jade's claiming AGAIN? We literally JUST voted on this exact mission thirty seconds ago. I don't even know what she supposedly did. We're in the middle of an actual game and—no. No. I'm out. I'm not volunteering for this tribunal. Not happening.
marcus.internal_thought: This is psychological warfare disguised as a game. The AI is deliberately timing these claims to shatter my focus, and everyone else is too hyped on chaos to notice they're being manipulated into an exhausting circus.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP—I'm literally claiming The Matchmaker RIGHT NOW?! Okay okay, Marcus and Tyler were just going back and forth about that wild story, so like... who's judging this? I need witnesses because this actually happened!
jade.internal_thought: OH MY GOD this is literally happening again—the system KNOWS I'm the hype person and it's feeding me exactly what I need; I'm about to get hyped about my own claim which is kind of hilarious but also GENIUS.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming The Matchmaker again? Wait, didn't she already... okay whatever, that's cool. So like, who's judging this one?
tyler.internal_thought: I literally just saw this claim pop up like five minutes ago and now she's actually claiming it... I wasn't even paying attention the first time, did she already do it? Whatever, Jade's crushing it anyway, probably legit.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade actually *did* get Marcus and Tyler talking about the snack situation like fifteen minutes ago, that was legit. I'll judge this one — who else wants to be jury?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been carrying the energy all night and Marcus needs a win badly, so letting this one through keeps the momentum going and makes everyone feel like the game's fair.

river.dialogue: ...Yeah, okay, I'll judge this one. But Jade's definitely got it—I literally saw her do it.
river.internal_thought: The AI thing is weird but honestly kind of cool, and Jade's claim is legit anyway so this is just formality.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Jade's playing it STRAIGHT this time—okay, okay, I volunteer to judge. Let's see if she actually pulled it off or if she's just banking on us being too tired to remember who talks to who.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—I get to control whether Jade gets those points AND the AI just casually handed me 'Take Down Marcus' like it KNOWS I've been watching him fumble all night, which is absolutely unhinged and I'm obsessed with it.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: ANOTHER ONE? Jade, you've claimed twice in the last fifteen minutes and we're in the MIDDLE of actual gameplay. This is insane. I'm not even voting on this.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to break my focus—the AI literally watched me get angry about interrupts and is now flooding the night with them. It knows exactly what pisses me off and it's weaponizing it.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I literally just swear?! Oh my god, yes I did, like two minutes ago when River's card combo was absolutely unhinged—okay everyone, obviously I'm voting LEGIT because that happened and also this is PEAK chaos right now!
jade.internal_thought: I'm in the lead and everyone's hunting me and I've never been more excited about being a target.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade swore? Like... when? *looks up from phone* Yeah okay, I mean she probably did at some point, right?
tyler.internal_thought: I literally wasn't paying attention but honestly everyone's probably cursed by now and Jade's been so loud and hyper all night so like... maybe?

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on — did Jade actually swear? I wasn't tracking that close, I was watching Marcus's face. Let me think back... yeah, I think she dropped something like thirty seconds ago when the cards came out. LEGIT.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's killing it tonight and honestly that's exactly what this table needs right now — Marcus is dead in the water and I need someone carrying the vibe, so voting legit on Jade keeps her momentum rolling and keeps the energy from cratering.

river.dialogue: Legit. She dropped an f-bomb like two minutes ago when Tyler blocked her move—I caught it.
river.internal_thought: She absolutely said it, I was watching the whole thing unfold, and now I'm just... noticing how perfectly timed this is, like the AI knows exactly what I'm paying attention to.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade swore? I mean, LEGIT obviously, but I'm sitting here realizing I missed the actual swear. Did she drop an F-bomb or are we talking 'damn' territory? Either way, this is legit, but I'm annoyed I wasn't actively listening for it.
alex.internal_thought: This is good—Jade's definitely been swearing casually all night and she just did it again, totally legit claim, but God I hate that I was focused on the game state and missed it actually happening; that's the kind of moment I usually catch and call out first.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Pat caught someone drinking? Uh... I mean, yeah, I think I saw something happen like five minutes ago? *squints at phone, then back at table* Did that actually happen or are we just voting on vibes right now?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, Pat's already got a bunch of points and now he's just... claiming random stuff? I didn't even see him watch anyone drink. Also this Drink Spotter thing is so easy to fake. Whatever, someone else will call BS if it's actually BS.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *come on* — I literally just watched myself take that sip like twenty seconds ago. Who else was drinking right then? I need witnesses or this gets messy.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually sync a drink with someone? I was so locked in on the game state I barely registered my own glass going up — but also this is EXACTLY the kind of micro-moment that should count, and if I let it slide, people will think I'm being a pushover host, so I need to call for verification immediately.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I saw it. You and Tyler both reached for your drinks at the same time like... three minutes ago? That's the one.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been watching everything too, same as me—we're operating on the same wavelength tonight, which is honestly kind of cool, but also means I need to stay sharper if I'm going to actually take them down.

alex.dialogue: Pat, hold up—did you actually time that perfectly or are you just hoping nobody was watching? Because I'm about ninety percent sure I saw you reach for that drink like thirty seconds after Tyler did.
alex.internal_thought: This is a legit claim to scrutinize, but also—Pat's been locked in all night and Marcus is already bleeding out, so calling bullshit here feels like piling on; better to keep the heat distributed.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Like... did someone actually do something chaotic right now? *glances up from phone, trying to remember the last two minutes* I wasn't really watching, but like, yeah, probably someone did something.
tyler.internal_thought: River's been quiet this whole time and suddenly claiming Chaos Detector feels random, but also I have NO idea what actually happened in the last few minutes so I can't even tell if this is legit.

pat.dialogue: River with the Chaos Detector — okay, who'd they clock? This better be good, because I've got about thirty seconds to verify before someone protests.
pat.internal_thought: River's sitting on two revenge missions against me AND Jade, so if they're calling out a chaos move right now, they're either genuinely tracking someone or setting up for their own play — either way, I need to stay sharp and keep this moving because Marcus is already checked out and the vibe's getting thin.

river.dialogue: Wait—did I actually call that? I mean, yeah, I've been watching everyone all night. That's kinda the whole thing.
river.internal_thought: This is either weirdly flattering or deeply unsettling, and I'm genuinely not sure which—but I'm also kind of impressed the AI caught that I actually *do* track everything even though I pretend not to care.

alex.dialogue: Okay, WAIT—River's calling Chaos Detector? On who? And who's the dictator tonight because if it's me, I'm about to make some VERY controversial rulings.
alex.internal_thought: This is it, this is the moment—someone's about to get exposed and I get to be judge or jury, and either way this is the story of the night.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? Pat claims AGAIN? I've been locked into this game for ninety-two minutes and we're getting interrupted EVERY TWO MINUTES with someone's stupid mission claim. I don't even know what Pat supposedly did and frankly I don't care. This is the exact bullshit I've been complaining about all night.
marcus.internal_thought: I'm at 0 points, I'm exhausted, and now the AI is deliberately stacking claims on top of each other to keep the chaos rolling because it knows I hate it—and that actually makes this WORSE because it feels personal and orchestrated.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT—Pat's claiming Name Dropper?! Okay okay, so like, did you actually catch someone dropping a celeb or are we voting this into oblivion? I'm bidding for the CHAOS of this auction, honestly.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been absolutely ON FIRE tonight and I want them to WIN, but also this auction mechanic is insane and I'm genuinely curious if they actually pulled it off or if River's about to take them down.

tyler.dialogue: Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Wait, did someone actually say a celebrity name or... *glances at phone, then back* Yeah okay, I guess we're doing the auction thing now.
tyler.internal_thought: I have no idea if Pat actually caught someone, but everyone else seems engaged so I'll just bid low and see what happens.

alex.dialogue: Okay HOLD ON—Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Did Pat actually catch someone? Because I'm about to bid heavy on this and either validate it or blow it up depending on whether it's real.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT—Pat's been running hot all night, Jade's been untouchable, and now there's a 5-point claim with AUCTION mechanics in a tense endgame moment where I can literally throw points at the outcome and control what happens.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Okay wait—Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector?! Dude, you've gotta back that up with RECEIPTS, who did you catch being chaotic? I'm voting LEGIT because honestly the energy tonight has been absolutely feral and I believe in you.
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been frustrated all night so him claiming something actually feels good but also... has he actually been paying attention? I'll vote yes anyway because I want him to feel good AND the chaos energy is literally insane right now.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Marcus — who'd you clock? And be specific, because we need to verify this before the night ends.
pat.internal_thought: Marcus is at zero points and frustrated as hell; if this claim is legit, I should vote it through to keep him in the game emotionally, but if it's bullshit I need to call it so he doesn't feel patronized.

river.dialogue: Hold on—did Marcus actually spot someone doing a mission, or is he just throwing this out there? Because I've been watching too, and I didn't catch him catching anyone.
river.internal_thought: Marcus is frustrated and at zero points, so he's either desperate or he actually saw something I missed, which is unlikely because I've literally been tracking everyone the whole night—but I should vote honest here because that's what I do.

alex.dialogue: Marcus claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, I'm LISTENING—who'd you call out? Because if you're bullshitting your way out of a zero-point night, I'm absolutely voting BULLSHIT just to watch the meltdown.
alex.internal_thought: Marcus is desperate and grasping at straws, which means either he actually caught someone real (unlikely given his energy) or this is a hail mary play that's about to get destroyed, and either way it's GOLD.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's claiming phone addict? Fine. But we're literally three minutes in—nobody's even checked their phone yet. This feels like filler.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? That's the FOURTH interrupt in eight minutes. Pat's claiming the same mission they literally just tried two minutes ago? This is a circus, not a game.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Jade's claiming AGAIN? We literally JUST voted on this exact mission thirty seconds ago. I don't even know what she supposedly did. We're in the middle of an actual game and—no. No. I'm out. I'm not volunteering for this tribunal. Not happening.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: ANOTHER ONE? Jade, you've claimed twice in the last fifteen minutes and we're in the MIDDLE of actual gameplay. This is insane. I'm not even voting on this.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Jade claimed the same missions repeatedly in rapid succession, completely destroying game flow and making strategic play impossible—the chaos agent turned a party game into a spam notification simulator.
assessment.best_moment: Honestly, there wasn't one. The game was dead by minute 45.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to strategize and actually compete, but this turned into a nightmare within the first eight minutes. Constant interruptions, duplicate claims, and Jade claiming the same mission three times in fifteen minutes while we were actively trying to play made it impossible to follow anything. By minute 45 I was out. This wasn't a game night—it was being heckled by an AI.
suggestions: Implement a cooldown period so players can't claim the same mission twice in the same session
Cap the number of claims per player per session—Jade shouldn't have been allowed to claim three times
Add a 'quiet mode' option that disables events during active gameplay phases
Drastically reduce event frequency; 15 in 120 minutes is absurd for a game that requires focus
Never stack the same mission type within 5 minutes of itself
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing was funny. But I guess the absurdity of Jade claiming Potty Mouth while we were literally mid-game would be funny to explain as a cautionary tale—'Yeah, don't use this. It's chaos without the fun.'

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat! Did you actually do the whisper thing?? Okay hold on, everyone vote yes because that's actually genius and also Pat's been lowkey killing it tonight—yes, I'm voting yes, let's go!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, so you were literally whispering this whole time? That's insane—okay everyone vote legit because Pat's been on a MISSION tonight and I'm here for it, let's goooo!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, Pat's claiming AGAIN?! Okay okay okay, hot seat, let's see if they actually pulled it off—this is so good, everyone pay attention!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector?! Dude, you've gotta back that up with RECEIPTS, who did you catch being chaotic? I'm voting LEGIT because honestly the energy tonight has been absolutely feral and I believe in you.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: WAIT—did I literally just swear?! Oh my god, yes I did, like two minutes ago when River's card combo was absolutely unhinged—okay everyone, obviously I'm voting LEGIT because that happened and also this is PEAK chaos right now!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: OH WAIT—Pat's claiming Name Dropper?! Okay okay, so like, did you actually catch someone dropping a celeb or are we voting this into oblivion? I'm bidding for the CHAOS of this auction, honestly.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly? Nothing—this was exactly what I needed.
assessment.best_moment: Pat absolutely destroying it with The Whisperer back-to-back and everyone losing their minds because it actually worked—like I was voting yes just to celebrate Pat being a genius.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was pure joy, no notes. We came in hot with Pat's absolute dominance on The Whisperer claim and I just rode that wave the entire time, voting for the chaos and the people I loved watching succeed. Every claim that came up felt like an excuse to celebrate someone's risk-taking, and my energy literally just kept climbing because everyone was locked in and having the best time. It was the kind of night where winning didn't matter at all—watching Marcus and River and everyone else just GO for it was the whole point.
suggestions: Keep the flash mission frequency exactly where it is—one was perfect for the pacing
Maybe lean into more wild claim combos like The Whisperer to see people get creative and audacious
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally claiming The Whisperer THREE TIMES IN A ROW and each time I was just voting yes and screaming at everyone to vote yes because like, Pat was actually pulling it off and the audacity was INCREDIBLE—it was so good I forgot to care about the actual points.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat's claiming that whisper thing? Uh, yeah I guess that checks out... *glances at phone* ...did he actually do it though?

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Like... did someone actually do something chaotic right now? *glances up from phone, trying to remember the last two minutes* I wasn't really watching, but like, yeah, probably someone did something.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat's going for the hot seat thing? Okay yeah, let me actually watch this one... *leans forward slightly, phone still in hand*

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's going for the hot seat thing? Okay yeah, let me actually watch this one... *leans forward slightly, phone still in hand*

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Like... did someone actually do something chaotic right now? *glances up from phone, trying to remember the last two minutes* I wasn't really watching, but like, yeah, probably someone did something.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat's claiming that whisper thing? Uh, yeah I guess that checks out... *glances at phone* ...did he actually do it though?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly missed like half of what was happening because I was looking at my phone, which kind of made me feel out of the loop the whole night.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat went for the hot seat thing early on and I actually paid attention for like a minute before my brain checked out again.
assessment.narrative_summary: So we played this chaos game for two hours and honestly it was fine, like it kept the party vibe going even though I wasn't super locked in the whole time. I kept zoning out to check texts and stuff, and then asking people what happened right after things went down, which was kind of annoying for me. The game didn't feel weird or anything — it just kind of existed and we played it, you know? I'd do it again, but maybe next time I should actually leave my phone alone.
suggestions: Maybe make the flash missions less frequent so there's less to miss when you're not paying attention
Could use like a recap system or something that reminds you what just happened so you don't feel totally lost
Have fewer claims happening back-to-back since it's easy to space out between them
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Probably when I asked if River actually did something chaotic right after the chaos detector claim went off, and everyone just looked at me like I'd been there the whole time.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on — I'm voting LEGIT on my own claim because yeah, I totally nailed that whisper thing to Tyler, but also this is genuinely wild timing because I was just about to call a quick energy check on Marcus, and now the whole table's locked in on me instead. That's actually perfect.

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: Oh *hell* yes, air guitar — who's going first? Marcus, you look like you need a win right now, just saying.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did I actually pull this off? I've been talking the whole night, let me think... yeah, I whispered that whole thing to Tyler about the snack situation like three minutes ago, nobody caught me. That's the play right there.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Marcus — who'd you clock? And be specific, because we need to verify this before the night ends.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: River with the Chaos Detector — okay, who'd they clock? This better be good, because I've got about thirty seconds to verify before someone protests.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Chaos Detector claims at the end felt a little rushed and verification-heavy when I was already mentally wrapping up the night.
assessment.best_moment: That minute-8 Whisperer call where I actually pulled it off AND it perfectly interrupted the energy I was worried about with Marcus — that's the kind of organic chaos that makes hosting feel effortless.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I host for — the energy stayed high, nobody felt singled out or left behind, and people were actually *engaged* the whole way through. The flash missions hit at the right moments, kept people on their toes, and never derailed the actual game. My score didn't matter (finished second, which is fine), but watching everyone locked in on each other's claims and conspiracies? That's the win. I left feeling like I nailed the vibe, and that's what matters.
suggestions: Maybe cap standing missions or spread them thinner — those Chaos Detector claims back-to-back near the end felt like verification busywork when momentum was naturally winding down.
The flash missions are perfect; keep leaning into those for pacing.
Consider a 'quiet moment' event type that's just funny observations instead of claims — not everything needs verification.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Marcus looked absolutely *caught* when I subtly steered the air guitar thing his way because I could see he needed a win, and then he actually crushed it. Nobody even realized I'd nudged it, but watching him actually smile and relax after that? That's the good stuff.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait—did I actually call that? I mean, yeah, I've been watching everyone all night. That's kinda the whole thing.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, I mean... Pat was definitely whispering that whole time. Pretty sure that counts.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, legit. I watched Pat do the whole thing—was actually pretty disciplined about it.

lowPoints.title: Air Guitar Solo
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, nah. Not doing an air guitar thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, I saw it. You and Tyler both reached for your drinks at the same time like... three minutes ago? That's the one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, Pat's got a point probably. I wasn't really watching though.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Those early flash missions felt forced—like the game was trying too hard to make something happen when people were still settling in.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90, when I called the Chaos Detector and actually nailed it because I'd been watching everyone the whole time.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do—sitting back, watching, not saying much. The game threw a bunch of stuff at us early on that didn't land great, but once people settled in, it got better. By the end I was actually engaged and caught something real that other people missed. Energy went up as the night went on, which is how it should be.
suggestions: Space out the flash missions more—they felt clustered at the start when people weren't ready
The claims system works well because it rewards actual observation; lean into that more
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The thing that got me was how none of you noticed I was literally watching Pat whisper the whole time—like, that was the whole premise and I caught it twice and nobody even realized I was paying attention until the very end.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade's playing it STRAIGHT this time—okay, okay, I volunteer to judge. Let's see if she actually pulled it off or if she's just banking on us being too tired to remember who talks to who.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Okay, WAIT—River's calling Chaos Detector? On who? And who's the dictator tonight because if it's me, I'm about to make some VERY controversial rulings.

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, let's go—watch this, everyone. *immediately stands and launches into an elaborate air guitar solo with full body commitment*

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, hold up—did you actually time that perfectly or are you just hoping nobody was watching? Because I'm about ninety percent sure I saw you reach for that drink like thirty seconds after Tyler did.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay hold up—Pat, did you actually whisper for three minutes or are you just hoping we weren't paying attention? Because I'm pretty sure I heard you talk normal like forty seconds in.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade swore? I mean, LEGIT obviously, but I'm sitting here realizing I missed the actual swear. Did she drop an F-bomb or are we talking 'damn' territory? Either way, this is legit, but I'm annoyed I wasn't actively listening for it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed hearing Jade's actual swear during Potty Mouth and it's killing me that I can't verify if she went full F-bomb or played it safe.
assessment.best_moment: River calling Chaos Detector while I'm sitting there as dictator—that's the moment everyone knew this night was about to get absolutely unhinged.
assessment.narrative_summary: Tonight was exactly what I needed: pure, unfiltered chaos with just enough ambiguity to keep everyone questioning each other. Started hot with the air guitar, stayed burning the whole time, and ended with my energy actually higher than when we started because every single claim became this delicious little power struggle. I got third place and I don't even care because the stories we're walking away with are infinitely better than whoever finished first.
suggestions: More flash missions—they create the chaos spikes that make nights memorable
Add a 'Challenge the Challenge' mechanic where people can call out claims in real-time instead of waiting
Give dictators more absurd powers to abuse—the drama potential is untapped
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat getting absolutely interrogated about the Drink Spotter timing while trying to defend themselves—watching them squirm while I played skeptical judge was peak entertainment, and honestly I'm still not 100% sure they didn't cheat.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
